relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Joseph fell upon his father’s face and wept.
Israel’s body was prepared in the Egyptian way.
Egypt mourned him seventy days.
Joseph asked Pharaoh’s house to honor his father’s oath.
Jacob had asked to be buried in Canaan, in his own burial place.
Pharaoh released Joseph to fulfill the oath.
Egypt’s elders went up with Joseph.
Joseph’s whole family went, leaving the little ones and flocks in Goshen.
A heavy camp of chariots and horsemen accompanied the mourning.
At Atad, they mourned Jacob seven days.
The land saw Egypt’s heavy mourning and named the place for it.
The sons did as Jacob commanded.
Jacob was buried in Machpelah with the fathers and mothers.
Joseph returned to Egypt after the burial.
With Jacob dead, the brothers feared Joseph’s revenge.
They sent a message invoking their father’s wish.
They asked forgiveness, and Joseph wept.
The brothers fell before him, calling themselves servants.
Joseph refused the place of God and told them not to fear.
What they planned for evil, the Divine planned for good, to keep many alive.
Joseph comforted them and promised to sustain their children.
Joseph lived in Egypt to one hundred ten years.
He saw generations of Ephraim and Manasseh.
Joseph trusted the future visitation and return to the promised land.
He made Israel’s sons swear to carry his bones when the Divine visited them.
Joseph died in Egypt, embalmed and placed in a coffin, while the promise waited.
